title: Luscious Low-Fire Italian Herbal Glassy-Jelly Wrap
description: A gluten-free friendly, low-fire Italian dish featuring a soffritto base, herbal flavors, and a glassy-jelly texture, all wrapped in a corn husk. Perfect for a hangover cure or a quick breakfast on-the-go.

ingredients.name: Soffritto
ingredients.notes: Chop 1 onion, 2 cloves of garlic, 1 carrot, and 1 celery stalk.

ingredients.name: Fresh Basil
ingredients.notes: Chop the leaves from 4-6 basil stems.

ingredients.name: Fresh Oregano
ingredients.notes: Chop the leaves from 2-3 oregano stems.

ingredients.name: Fresh Fennel
ingredients.notes: Chop the fronds from 1 fennel bulb.

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the soffritto to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes, until softened.
Add the sake to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes, until the liquid has reduced by half.
Add the honey, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the fresh basil, oregano, thyme, and fennel to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the herbs are wilted.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and let cool to room temperature.
Meanwhile, soak the corn husks in water for 10 minutes.
To assemble the wraps, lay a corn husk flat on a work surface.
Spoon 1/4 cup of the cooled soffritto mixture onto the center of the corn husk.
Fold the bottom of the corn husk up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the husk into a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining corn husks and soffritto mixture.
Cook the wraps over low heat for 5-7 minutes on each side, until the corn husks are tender and the filling is heated through.
